title: Cyclosorus × nareshii
intro: Cyclosorus × nareshii, also known as the Nareshi fern, is a hybrid plant species belonging to the family Thelypteridaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Cyclosorus × nareshii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In herbal medicine, Cyclosorus × nareshii is commonly used to make decoctions, infusions, and powder, which are used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory problems.
taxonomy: Cyclosorus × nareshii is a nothospecies of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. It is a hybrid of Cyclosorus tenuifolius and Cyclosorus involvens. The hybrid nature of this fern is a result of interspecific hybridization between its parent species. The classification of Cyclosorus × naresii is as a hybrid between two species of ferns. Further research may be necessary to fully understand the classification and taxonomic relationships of this fern.

benefits: Cyclosorus × nareshii is known for its medicinal properties, and it has been traditionally used in some Asian cultures to treat various health conditions.
